sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Judy Gomez, a 61-year-old female, presents for ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
She reports being on a reduced dose of prednisone, 1 tablet daily, which she finds sufficient to manage her symptoms.
She mentions occasional exacerbation of joint pain in her feet, particularly when she has a day off and is more active.
She has been experiencing some bruising, which she attributes to the prednisone.
She also reports improved joint pain since starting methotrexate.
Past medical history: Psoriatic arthritis, history of COVID-19 vaccination, current treatment with methotrexate.
Review of systems: None mentioned.
Current medications: Prednisone 1 tablet daily for psoriatic arthritis.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has a good range of motion in the knee and toes.
The patient has ridges in her nails, consistent with psoriasis.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is a 61-year-old female with ongoing psoriatic arthritis, showing improvement with methotrexate and a plan to discontinue prednisone.
Problem list: Psoriatic arthritis; active.
Plan: I will discontinue prednisone and continue with methotrexate.
I will prescribe the patient to stop by the front desk to make an appointment for blood work.
I will see the patient in three months for follow-up.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in three months for follow-up.
